metadata.description: Threat actor attribution dataset for edge security vendors. Covers named APT groups, campaigns, and nation-state attributions linked to exploitation of edge/perimeter devices (firewalls, VPN gateways, ADCs) from 11 vendors.
metadata.methodology: Compiled from vendor docs in this repo, cross-referenced and expanded via Mandiant, CrowdStrike, CISA, Volexity, Recorded Future, Sophos X-Ops, Cisco Talos, Unit 42, and Arctic Wolf publications. Attribution confidence levels noted where sources provide them.
metadata.key_statistic: Mandiant M-Trends 2025: exploits remain the dominant initial access vector at 33% of all investigated incidents; all four most frequently exploited vulnerabilities in 2024 were in edge devices.

actors.name: UNC3886
actors.description: China-nexus espionage group specializing in edge device and hypervisor exploitation. Active since at least late 2021. Deploys custom TINYSHELL-based backdoors and has demonstrated deep knowledge of FortiOS, Junos OS, and VMware ESXi internals.
campaigns: FortiOS zero-day exploitation with BOLDMOVE-adjacent tooling
Juniper MX Series backdoor campaign (6 custom TINYSHELL variants, disclosed March 2025)
VMware ESXi hypervisor compromise

actors.name: UNC5221
actors.description: China-nexus espionage actor with persistent focus on Ivanti products. Responsible for the January 2024 Ivanti crisis and subsequent campaigns through 2025. Also linked to the F5 BIG-IP corporate breach (source code exfiltration) via BRICKSTORM malware.
campaigns: Ivanti Connect Secure mass exploitation (Jan 2024, ~1700 appliances compromised)
CISA Emergency Directive ED 24-01
CVE-2025-0282 exploitation (Dec 2024 onward, SPAWN/DRYHOOK/PHASEJAM malware)
CVE-2025-22457 exploitation (Mar 2025, TRAILBLAZE/BRUSHFIRE malware)
F5 BIG-IP corporate intrusion (Aug 2025, source code + undisclosed vulns exfiltrated, BRICKSTORM malware)

actors.name: UNC5174
actors.description: Former member of Chinese hacktivist collectives, assessed as acting as a contractor for China's Ministry of State Security (MSS). Functions as an initial access broker, selling access to compromised systems.
campaigns: F5 BIG-IP CVE-2023-46747 exploitation (Oct-Nov 2023)
Attempted sale of access to US defense contractor and UK government appliances

actors.name: UAT4356
actors.description: State-sponsored espionage group with deep specialization in Cisco ASA/FTD products. Conducted the ArcaneDoor campaign and returned in 2025 with new zero-days. Censys research found links to Chinese networks and anti-censorship software on actor infrastructure.
campaigns: ArcaneDoor (Nov 2023 - Apr 2024, Line Dancer + Line Runner implants)
2025 ASA/FTD campaign (Sep 2025, RayInitiator bootkit + LINE VIPER shellcode loader)

actors.name: UTA0218
actors.description: State-nexus threat actor responsible for Operation MidnightEclipse, exploiting Palo Alto PAN-OS GlobalProtect zero-day CVE-2024-3400. Volexity assesses with moderate confidence the actor is China-based.
campaigns: Operation MidnightEclipse (Mar-Apr 2024, UPSTYLE backdoor)

actors.name: Volt Typhoon
actors.description: PRC state-sponsored group focused on pre-positioning in US critical infrastructure for potential disruption during a conflict. Uses living-off-the-land techniques post-compromise. Exploits edge devices from multiple vendors for initial access.
campaigns: US critical infrastructure pre-positioning (ongoing since at least 2021)
Exploitation of Fortinet FortiOS SSL VPN and Ivanti Connect Secure VPN
Sophos Pacific Rim (identified as overlapping TTP cluster)

actors.name: APT41
actors.description: Dual-purpose Chinese threat group conducting both state-sponsored espionage and financially motivated operations. Exploited Citrix CVE-2019-19781 in a wide-ranging global intrusion campaign in early 2020. Also linked to Sophos firewall targeting via Pacific Rim.
campaigns: Global intrusion campaign exploiting Citrix, Cisco, and Zoho (Jan-Mar 2020)
Sophos Pacific Rim (identified as overlapping TTP cluster)

actors.name: APT31
actors.description: China-linked espionage group that exploited Sophos CyberoamOS vulnerability CVE-2020-29574 and was identified as part of the Pacific Rim threat cluster targeting Sophos edge devices.
campaigns: CyberoamOS exploitation
Sophos Pacific Rim (identified as overlapping TTP cluster)

actors.name: DriftingCloud
actors.description: Chinese APT group that exploited Sophos Firewall CVE-2022-1040 zero-day weeks before the patch was available. Used compromised firewalls to perform MitM attacks by modifying DNS responses.

geographies: South Asia (Afghanistan, Bhutan, India, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka)

campaigns: Sophos Firewall zero-day exploitation with DNS MitM (Mar 2022)

actors.name: TA413
actors.description: China-nexus group targeting Tibetan government-in-exile organizations. Exploited Sophos CVE-2022-1040 to deploy the custom LOWZERO backdoor through compromised firewalls.
campaigns: Tibetan government targeting via Sophos firewall compromise (2022)

actors.name: Pioneer Kitten
actors.description: Iran-based cyber actor associated with the Government of Iran. Exploited Check Point CVE-2024-24919 and Fortinet VPN vulnerabilities for initial access, then collaborates with ransomware affiliates (AlphV, RansomHouse, NoEscape) for monetization.
campaigns: Check Point VPN zero-day exploitation (May-Aug 2024)
Fortinet VPN credential harvesting for ransomware enablement
Collaboration with AlphV/BlackCat, RansomHouse, and NoEscape ransomware operators
sources: Department of Defense Cyber Crime Center (DC3)

actors.name: BOLDMOVE Actor (unnamed)
actors.description: Suspected China-nexus actor that developed custom BOLDMOVE malware specifically designed for FortiGate firewalls. Exploited CVE-2022-42475 as a zero-day targeting European government and African managed service provider.
campaigns: FortiOS SSL-VPN zero-day exploitation with BOLDMOVE backdoor (Oct 2022 onward)

actors.name: LockBit
actors.description: Prolific ransomware-as-a-service operation. Affiliates exploited Citrix CVE-2023-4966 (CitrixBleed) in high-profile attacks on Boeing, ICBC, DP World, and Allen & Overy. Also targeted Fortinet VPN infrastructure.
campaigns: CitrixBleed exploitation (Oct-Nov 2023): Boeing, ICBC (disrupted US Treasury markets), DP World, Allen & Overy
CISA/FBI/ACSC joint advisory AA23-325A

actors.name: Akira
actors.description: Ransomware group that systematically exploited SonicWall SSL VPN CVE-2024-40766 for initial access. Responsible for approximately 75% of the documented SonicWall ransomware intrusions.
campaigns: SonicWall SSL VPN mass exploitation (Sep 2024 - Aug 2025, 30+ documented intrusions per Arctic Wolf)

actors.name: Fog
actors.description: Ransomware group that exploited SonicWall SSL VPN CVE-2024-40766 alongside Akira, responsible for approximately 25% of the documented SonicWall intrusions.
campaigns: SonicWall SSL VPN exploitation (Sep 2024 onward)

actors.name: Helldown
actors.description: Ransomware group employing double extortion that weaponized Zyxel CVE-2024-11667 path traversal to steal VPN credentials and establish backdoor connections.
campaigns: Zyxel firewall exploitation (Aug 2024 onward, 31 victims listed on extortion portal)

actors.name: Cring
actors.description: Ransomware group that specifically targeted unpatched Fortinet FortiGate VPN servers via CVE-2018-13379 to gain initial access to industrial and enterprise networks.
campaigns: FortiGate VPN exploitation for ransomware deployment (2021)

actors.name: UNC5027
actors.description: Threat cluster tracked by Mandiant responsible for backdooring at least 2,000 NetScaler devices before a patch existed for CVE-2023-3519. Planted webshells and PHP backdoors for persistent access.
campaigns: NetScaler ADC zero-day mass compromise (Jul 2023, 2000+ devices backdoored pre-patch)

actors.name: Earth Estries
actors.description: Conducts the largest documented edge device campaign, breaching over 600 organizations across 80 countries since 2019. Heavy focus on telecommunications providers including multiple US carriers (October 2024).

geographies: global (80+ countries), concentrated US telecom targeting

campaigns: US telecom provider breaches (Oct 2024)
Broad edge device exploitation campaign (2019-present, 600+ organizations)

actors.name: Sandworm-linked actors
actors.description: Tooling associated with Russia's GRU Unit 74455 was linked to exploitation of Zyxel CVE-2022-30525 for establishing persistent access to edge devices. The exact operational unit may be a subcluster or affiliate.
campaigns: Zyxel firewall exploitation with Sandworm-associated tooling (2022)

actors.name: MySonicWall State Actor (unnamed)
actors.description: Unnamed state-sponsored threat actor that breached SonicWall's MySonicWall cloud backup service, exfiltrating firewall configuration data for all cloud backup customers. SonicWall did not disclose the nation-state attribution.
campaigns: MySonicWall cloud backup breach (Sep 2025, initially claimed <5% affected, revised to 100%)
Downstream: Marquis Software ransomware attack affecting 74 US banks (Aug 2025)

actors.name: Mirai botnet operators
actors.description: Multiple Mirai-variant botnet operators mass-exploited Zyxel firewall vulnerabilities in 2023 for DDoS recruitment. Represents the commodity/botnet threat tier distinct from nation-state and ransomware actors.
campaigns: Zyxel firewall DDoS botnet recruitment (Spring 2023, ~24,500 exposed devices per Censys)

Fortinet.notes: Most targeted edge vendor by volume. China-nexus actors (UNC3886, Volt Typhoon) and Iran-nexus (Pioneer Kitten) both actively exploit. 87,000 VPN credentials leaked from unpatched CVE-2018-13379 devices. BOLDMOVE malware purpose-built for FortiOS internals.

Ivanti.notes: Triggered CISA Emergency Directive ED 24-01 (Jan 2024). UNC5221 is the primary persistent threat, returning with new exploits across 2024-2025. ~1,700 appliances compromised in initial Jan 2024 wave. Ivanti ICT integrity checker defeated by sophisticated actors; CISA found factory resets insufficient.

Cisco.notes: UAT4356 has burned 4 ASA/FTD zero-days across two campaigns (2024, 2025) with iterating implant toolset (Line Dancer/Runner, RayInitiator/LINE VIPER). Censys found links to Chinese networks. ArcaneDoor is a MITRE ATT&CK-cataloged campaign (C0046).

Palo Alto Networks.notes: CVE-2024-3400 (CVSS 10.0) was the #1 most frequently exploited vulnerability in Mandiant M-Trends 2025. Operation MidnightEclipse deployed UPSTYLE backdoor. Discovered by Volexity in active exploitation before any patch existed.

nation_states: unknown (state-sponsored per Mandiant for MySonicWall breach)
SonicWall.notes: Dual failure mode: (1) CVE-2024-40766 enabled sustained Akira/Fog ransomware campaign (30+ intrusions, full encryption in <10 hours). (2) MySonicWall cloud backup breach by state actor exposed all backup customers, leading to downstream Marquis attack on 74 US banks. Marquis filed suit Feb 2026.

Sophos.notes: Highest documented rate of state-sponsored exploitation among all vendors. Pacific Rim report (Oct 2024) documented 5-year campaign by multiple interlinked China-nexus groups. Exploit development traced to Sichuan region. Targets included nuclear energy suppliers, airports, military hospitals, government ministries. At least 4 of 6 KEV entries linked to China-nexus actors.

Zyxel.notes: Full-spectrum threat actor coverage: nation-state (Sandworm-linked), ransomware (Helldown), and commodity botnets (Mirai). CVE-2022-30525 was silently patched without CVE/advisory. Hardcoded 'zyfwp' credential (CVE-2020-29583) is an egregious design failure.

nation_states: Russia (various actors per CISA/NSA joint advisory)
Citrix.notes: CVE-2019-19781 had a 33-day unpatched window; one of the most impactful edge CVEs ever. Over 80,000 companies vulnerable at peak. CitrixBleed (CVE-2023-4966) was weaponized by LockBit against Boeing and ICBC (disrupted US Treasury markets). APT41 exploited CVE-2019-19781 in Jan 2020 global campaign. UNC5027 backdoored 2,000+ devices pre-patch via CVE-2023-3519.

Juniper.notes: UNC3886 deployed 6 custom TINYSHELL-based backdoors on carrier-grade MX Series routers (disclosed Mar 2025). Targets included ISPs, telecoms, and US defense contractors. 2023 J-Web chain went from advisory to mass exploitation in 8 days. End-of-life hardware compounds risk.

F5.notes: F5 corporate breach (Aug 2025): nation-state actor (linked to China/UNC5221 via BRICKSTORM malware) exfiltrated BIG-IP source code and undisclosed vulnerability information after 12+ months of persistent access. CISA issued Emergency Directive ED 26-01 requiring federal inventory and patching. CVE-2022-1388 went from PoC to mass compromise in 48 hours. UNC5174 sold access to compromised F5 appliances.

Check Point.notes: Lowest edge KEV count (2) but CVE-2024-24919 enabled Iran-nexus lateral movement across thousands of enterprises. Pioneer Kitten collaborates with ransomware affiliates (AlphV, RansomHouse, NoEscape). 10,000+ devices actively scanned within 48 hours of PoC. FBI/CISA/DC3 joint advisory AA24-241A.

China.assessment: Dominant nation-state threat to edge devices. At least 12 distinct actor clusters targeting 8 of 11 vendors. Exploit development traced to Sichuan region (Sophos Pacific Rim). Ranges from espionage pre-positioning (Volt Typhoon) to active data exfiltration (UNC5221 F5 breach) to initial access brokering (UNC5174).

actors: Pioneer Kitten (Fox Kitten / Lemon Sandstorm / UNC757)
Iran.assessment: Primary focus on credential harvesting from VPN gateways. Distinctive model: state-sponsored actors monetize access by collaborating with ransomware affiliates (AlphV, RansomHouse, NoEscape).

Russia.assessment: Less dominant in edge device exploitation than China. Sandworm-linked tooling used against Zyxel. LockBit (Russian-origin RaaS) affiliates heavily exploited Citrix. Multiple Russian actors cited in CISA/NSA joint advisory on Citrix CVE-2019-19781 exploitation.
